mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in retail sales signals stronger near-term household demand and revenue expectations, increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot RSAFS was 486553.0 as of 2017-10
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce positive 20-day return Rank IC above 0.03 in walk-forward tests, or if top-quintile RSAFS momentum underperforms bottom-quintile momentum over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month average and rises at least 25 basis points over 3 months, tighter discount rates reduce equity valuation multiples and produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average and rises at least 0.25pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot FEDFUNDS was 1.15 as of 2017-10
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional mean returns with p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI inflation combined with rising FEDFUNDS signals policy pressure without a growth offset, raising discount-rate risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS rises over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot CPIAUCSL was 246.639 and FEDFUNDS was 1.15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger does not lower average 20-day returns versus periods with only one condition active, or if the interaction coefficient is nonnegative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate paired with decelerating payroll growth identifies late-cycle labor-market exhaustion, reducing expected earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.5% and PAYEMS 3-month average growth below its prior 6-month average growth
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot UNRATE was 4.1 and PAYEMS was 147010.0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered late-cycle labor observations generate positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if PAYEMS deceleration adds no explanatory power conditional on UNRATE below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month momentum in industrial production indicates improving output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot INDPRO was 104.6367 as of 2017-09
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 3-month momentum has Rank IC below 0.02 for 5-day returns across walk-forward windows or if positive INDPRO momentum is followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in housing starts signals weaker construction demand and household formation, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot HOUST was 1127.0 as of 2017-09
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST shortfalls greater than 5% below the 12-month average are not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the triggered return differential is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot UNRATE was 4.1 as of 2017-10
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases greater than 0.3pp over 3 months are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth confirmed by positive retail sales momentum signals broad demand strength, improving earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth exceeds 1.5% and RSAFS 3-month growth is positive
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot GDPC1 was 17156.946 and RSAFS was 486553.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 and RSAFS trigger does not outperform cases with only one positive demand signal over 60 trading days, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.
